title: UI-128 — Foundation navigation manifest (core module nav data + types)
prompt:
# UI-128 — Foundation navigation manifest

Wave: `app-shell-foundation-20260520` (00-foundation).
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Provide a single typed source of truth for the desktop AppSidebar groups/items and for the Settings sub-nav, so UI-129 and UI-132 do not duplicate copy or hard-code routes. This is a data/contract task — no JSX, no styling.

## Source of truth
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/shell.jsx:3-21` (`SSidebar`): groups Core / Operations / QA & Shipping / Premium.
- Cross-ref the active-item placement in every `<module>/shell.jsx`, especially `reporting/shell.jsx:3-23` and `multi-site/shell.jsx:77-124`, so the manifest covers the full 16-module MON set, not only the older 12-item sidebar.
- `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/shell.jsx:35-59` + the `window.SETTINGS_NAV` array referenced there (search prototypes/data*.jsx if needed).
- Brief: `/tmp/monopilot-shell-gap-brief-combined.md` §1 (snapshot), §3 Luka B/E.

## Files (scope)
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/module-registry.ts` [create — exports `APP_MODULES: AppModule[]` and every 16-module shell/nav decision]
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/app-nav.ts` [create — exports `APP_NAV_GROUPS: AppNavGroup[]` derived from sidebar-exposed APP_MODULES]
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/settings-nav.ts` [create — exports `SETTINGS_NAV_GROUPS: SettingsNavGroup[]`]
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/types.ts` [create — `AppModule`, `AppNavGroup`, `AppNavItem`, `SettingsNavGroup`, `SettingsNavItem` with `module_id`, `module_kind` (`platform|desktop|scanner`), `shell_kind` (`none|app|scanner`), `nav_exposure` (`sidebar|excluded|merged`), `merged_into`, `i18n_key`, `route`, `icon_token`, `count_slot` (always `null` here), `permission_key` (always `null` here), `rbac_todo` flag, `highlight` flag]
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/__tests__/app-nav.test.ts` [create]
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/__tests__/settings-nav.test.ts` [create]
- `apps/web/i18n/en.json` [modify — add `Navigation.app.*` and `Navigation.settings.*` keys; values from prototype labels]
- `apps/web/i18n/pl.json` [modify — same keys translated]
- `apps/web/i18n/uk.json` [modify — same keys translated; no missing keys]
- `apps/web/i18n/ro.json` [modify — same keys translated; no missing keys]

Do NOT modify proxy.ts, [locale]/layout.tsx, or any T-XXX.json outside this wave.

## Contract
`APP_MODULES` is the complete 16-module shell registry. It MUST contain exactly these module ids and decisions:

- `foundation`: `module_kind=platform`, `shell_kind=none`, `nav_exposure=excluded`, no sidebar route.
- `settings`: desktop sidebar item `Settings`, route `/settings`.
- `npd`: desktop sidebar item `NPD`, route `/npd`.
- `technical`: desktop sidebar item `Technical`, route `/technical`.
- `planning-basic`: desktop sidebar item `Planning`, route `/planning`.
- `planning-ext`: desktop sidebar item `Scheduler`, route `/scheduler`, label key `Navigation.app.scheduler` (Planning+ / Scheduler premium extension).
- `warehouse`: desktop sidebar item `Warehouse`, route `/warehouse`.
- `production`: desktop sidebar item `Production`, route `/production`.
- `quality`: desktop sidebar item `Quality`, route `/quality`.
- `finance`: desktop sidebar item `Finance`, route `/finance`.
- `shipping`: desktop sidebar item `Shipping`, route `/shipping`.
- `reporting`: desktop sidebar item `Reporting`, route `/reporting`, source `reporting/shell.jsx:21-23`.
- `maintenance`: desktop sidebar item `Maintenance`, route `/maintenance`.
- `multi-site`: desktop sidebar item `Multi-Site`, route `/multi-site`, source `multi-site/shell.jsx:101-122`; count/setup badges are slots only.
- `oee`: desktop sidebar item `OEE`, route `/oee`.
- `scanner`: `module_kind=scanner`, `shell_kind=scanner`, `nav_exposure=excluded`, not rendered in AppSidebar; T-134 owns `/dev/scanner` harness and future scanner module owns production scanner routes.

`APP_NAV_GROUPS` is derived from the 15 sidebar-exposed items (Dashboard + 14 desktop modules; excluding `foundation` and `scanner`, with `planning-basic` and `planning-ext` both explicit). Groups must be exactly:

- `Core`: Dashboard, Settings
- `Operations`: Planning, Scheduler, Production, Warehouse
- `QA & Shipping`: Quality, Shipping
- `Premium`: Technical, NPD, Finance, OEE, Maintenance
- `Analytics & Network`: Reporting, Multi-Site

All routes are locale-relative (for example `/settings`, never `/en/settings`); consumers add `/${locale}`.

Each item shape:
```ts
{ module_id: 'settings', key: 'settings', i18n_key: 'Navigation.app.settings', route: '/settings', icon_token: 'cog', count_slot: null, permission_key: null, rbac_todo: 'gate by 02-settings/T-130 after RBAC enum lock' }
```

Settings sub-nav items match `window.SETTINGS_NAV` in the prototype (Users, Roles, Authorization, Invitations, Security, Profile, Schema, Reference, …) with the same group labels.


## Documented deviations from literal prototype labels
These are intentional IA decisions for the unified product shell and MUST NOT be treated as parity drift:

- `planning-ext` is exposed as `Scheduler` at `/scheduler`; planning-ext prototype copy also uses `Planning+` for settings/PRO surfaces. The shell label uses Scheduler because PRD 07 and screen breadcrumbs centre the Scheduler workflow.
- `maintenance` is placed under `Premium`, not Operations, to keep the paid-module grouping consistent with current module glossary even though maintenance/shell.jsx shows it near Operations in its local module shell.
- `Reporting` and `Multi-Site` are grouped under synthesized `Analytics & Network`; reporting/shell.jsx uses `Analytics` and multi-site/shell.jsx uses `NEW`. The unified shell avoids two one-item groups while preserving both items and their prototype visual/badge semantics.
- `Dashboard` is a shell-level Core item, not one of the 16 module ids; `foundation` remains platform/non-user-facing and excluded.

## Acceptance criteria
1. `APP_MODULES` covers all 16 MON modules with explicit platform/desktop/scanner decisions, and `APP_NAV_GROUPS` has exactly 5 groups / 15 sidebar items in declared order, including Scheduler, Reporting, and Multi-Site.
2. `SETTINGS_NAV_GROUPS` matches the prototype `SETTINGS_NAV` content 1:1 (item count, order, labels, icons, highlights).
3. Every item carries a unique `i18n_key` resolvable in all runtime locale files: `apps/web/i18n/{en,pl,uk,ro}.json`.
4. RTL/unit tests assert: 16-module matrix, sidebar count, key uniqueness, route uniqueness, no hard-coded locale prefix, scanner/foundation explicit exclusion, `count_slot === null` on every sidebar item, `permission_key === null` on every item, and `rbac_todo` non-empty for every item that would later be RBAC-gated.
5. `pnpm --filter web test app-nav settings-nav -- --run` passes; `pnpm --filter web typecheck` passes; `pnpm --filter web lint` passes.

## Out of scope
- Rendering (UI-129 sidebar, UI-132 settings subnav).
- Permissions enum / RBAC (02-settings/T-130).
- Module-specific module sub-navs (PlanNav, ProdNav, WhNav, ...).
- Topbar links.

## RED test
Create `app-nav.test.ts` and `settings-nav.test.ts` first, failing because exports do not exist; then implement.

## Risk red lines
- Never hard-code a locale prefix into a route field.
- Never gate items here — emit `rbac_todo` and leave `permission_key: null`.
- Do not import this file from server-only modules without `'server-only'` consideration (data is plain JSON; safe everywhere).

## Closeout
Provide: acceptance_criteria_status, test_commands_and_results, changed_files, git_status, deviations_from_prototype (note any item label that differs from the literal prototype label and why).

pipeline_inputs.description: Typed module registry + nav manifest for the full MonoPilot shell: all 16 modules get explicit shell/nav decisions; AppSidebar exposes 15 desktop items (Dashboard + desktop modules), while foundation and scanner are intentionally excluded from desktop sidebar.
pipeline_inputs.details: Create APP_MODULES as the complete 16-module shell registry, then derive APP_NAV_GROUPS from sidebar-exposed modules. Include Scheduler (planning-ext), Reporting, and Multi-Site; classify foundation as platform/non-user-facing and scanner as scanner shell/excluded from desktop nav. Keep count_slot=null and permission_key=null, with rbac_todo strings for future gates. Add i18n keys in apps/web/i18n/{en,pl,uk,ro}.json. Tests assert complete module coverage, group/order, route uniqueness, locale-relative routes, and settings subnav parity. Documented deviations: planning-ext shell label is Scheduler (prototype also says Planning+); Maintenance grouped under Premium; Reporting+Multi-Site grouped as Analytics & Network; Dashboard is shell-level, foundation is platform-only.
acceptance_criteria: APP_MODULES contains exactly the 16 MON modules (foundation, settings, npd, technical, planning-basic, warehouse, scanner, planning-ext, production, quality, finance, shipping, reporting, maintenance, multi-site, oee) with explicit module_kind/shell_kind/nav_exposure decisions

APP_NAV_GROUPS has 5 groups in order Core/Operations/QA & Shipping/Premium/Analytics & Network with 15 sidebar items, including Scheduler, Reporting, and Multi-Site, and excluding foundation/scanner by explicit decision
SETTINGS_NAV_GROUPS matches the prototype SETTINGS_NAV 1:1 in count, order, labels, icons, highlights
Every sidebar/subnav item has a unique i18n_key resolvable in apps/web/i18n/{en,pl,uk,ro}.json; all route values are locale-relative and unique
All count_slot=null, permission_key=null, rbac_todo non-empty for future-RBAC items
pnpm --filter web test app-nav settings-nav -- --run passes; typecheck and lint pass
test_strategy: Write app-nav.test.ts and settings-nav.test.ts asserting APP_MODULES complete 16-module matrix first (RED)
Assert APP_NAV_GROUPS exposes 15 sidebar items and excludes foundation/scanner with explicit decisions
Assert route uniqueness and locale-relative route shape for every sidebar item
risk_red_lines: Do not hard-code a locale prefix into route fields
Do not gate items by permissions — UI-128 has no RBAC awareness
Do not import server-only modules from these files
Do not omit Reporting, Multi-Site, or planning-ext/Scheduler from the registry
Do not render scanner in the desktop AppSidebar; scanner uses the (scanner) shell only
Do not create routes in this task; UI-138 owns route stubs/contracts for active nav links
